title: -Contract: Bayview Industrial Services, Inc. for Removal of Transfer Station Underground Storage Tanks Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for Removal of Transfer Station Underground
Storage Tanks; and
2. Accepting the bid of Bayview Industrial Services, Inc. as the lowest, responsive
and responsible bidder; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions, or change orders until the Project is completed in accordance with
the approved plans and specifications for an amount not to exceed $292,200,
which includes a contingency of $48,360.

sections.current_situation:
The Transfer Station provides on-site fueling for City vehicles. Currently, there are two
(2) single-walled 10,000-gallon underground storage tanks (UST) for diesel on site. The
California Water Board, under the authority of Senate Bill 445 signed on September 25,
2014, mandated that all single-walled underground storage tanks (USTs) and non-
exempt single-walled piping be permanently closed by December 31, 2025. Systems
not closed by the deadline will be considered out of compliance and will be subject to
enforcement. Enforcement actions include civil penalties of $500 to $5,000 per day per

for Removal of Underground Storage Tanks September 9, 2025
tank for ongoing non-compliance beginning January 1, 2026. The scope of this Removal
of Transfer Station Underground Storage Tanks Project (project) includes removing two
single-walled 10,000-gallon underground storage tanks at the transfer station to comply
with the state law.
Requests for informal bids were sent to a list of qualified contractors on July 17, 2025.
Since the engineer’s estimate was under $200,000, the project was not advertised with
a formal RFP. Bids were opened on August 13, 2025. The City received three bids
ranging from $241,840 to $427,892.63. The lowest responsive and responsible bidder
was determined to be Bayview Industrial Services, Inc. Staff recommends that a
construction contract for the Project be awarded to Bayview Industrial Services, Inc.
The Transfer Station is located at 1201 Second Street. The Transfer Station serves
multiple waste management and resource recovery functions for residents and some
commercial users. Core functions of the Transfer Station include: accepting refuse for
landfill, recycling, and composting organic material.
sections.background:
The Transfer Station was constructed and began operation in 1983. It replaced the
previous City landfill and has since served as the City's primary location for solid waste
transfer and recycling operations. There are two 10,000 gallon, single-walled
underground diesel storage tanks on site that need to be removed per Water Board
regulation.
Public Works engaged with the services of an architectural consultant, structural
engineer, and environmental consultant in 2024 to create the bid documents for this
project.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This project will address the City’s sustainability and climate goals by reducing the risk of
tank leaks into the surrounding soil/groundwater. There are no anticipated negative
environmental effects of this action.
sections.rationale:
Although Bayview Industrial Services, Inc.’s bid exceeded the engineer’s estimate, the
contractor was still selected. If the City rebid the project, the schedule would be
jeopardized such that the tank would not be removed by the January 1, 2026 deadline.
As a result, the City would be responsible for paying fines imposed by the Regional Water
Quality Control Board in addition to being responsible for removing the tanks. The City
does not have the in-house expertise to perform this highly specialized work. In addition
to addressing regulatory compliance concerns, contracting this work is necessary to meet
state-mandated requirements under the applicable Senate Bill, which requires timely
removal and replacement of non-compliant underground storage tanks.

for Removal of Underground Storage Tanks September 9, 2025
sections.alternatives:
The City may choose not to proceed with removal and replacement of the mandated
double-walled tanks. However, deferring the work could expose the City to significant
daily fines, ranging from $500 to $5,000, imposed by local enforcement agencies or the
Regional Water Quality Control Board. In addition, postponement would place the City
out of compliance with state water quality regulations and could lead to increased
regulatory scrutiny and potential enforcement actions.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Department of Public Works (510) 981-6396

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###‑N.S.
CONTRACT: BAYVIEW INDUSTRIAL SERVICES, INC. FOR REMOVAL OF
TRANSFER STATION UNDERGROUND STORAGE TANKS; 1201 SECOND STREET
WHEREAS, to meet the California Water Quality Control Board requirements, the City
requires the removal two 10,000-gallon undergrounds storage tanks (USTs), associated
piping, and fueling equipment per Plans and Specifications; and
WHEREAS, the California Water Quality Control Board requires that all single-walled
USTs and associated single-walled piping must be permanently closed and removed from
service by December 31, 2025, or be subject to enforcement actions including civil
penalties; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
project; and
WHEREAS, informal bids (Plans and Specifications) were duly requested, and Bayview
Industrial Services, Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive and responsible
bidder.
WHEREAS, funds are available in the FY 2026 budget in the Zero Waste Fund.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specifications for Removal of Transfer Station Underground Storage Tanks
Project are approved.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley accepts the bid of
Bayview Industrial Services, Inc. as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager, or designee, to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions or change
orders, until completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans and
specifications with (Bayview Industrial Services, Inc.) for the Removal of Transfer Station
Underground Storage Tanks Project in an amount not to exceed $292,200, which
includes a contingency in the amount of $48,360. A record signature copy of the
agreement and any amendments will be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
